title: API keys flow through raw String interpolation with no redaction newtype
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access

Location: src/proxy.rs line 1390 (forward_to_provider) and line 1413 (set_auth_header), and src/providers/openai.rs line 80, qwen.rs line 69, deepseek.rs line 67, zai.rs line 85.

Violation: API keys are inlined into Authorization header values via format!("Bearer {api_key}") and into x-api-key via raw api_key strings (line 1387, 1410). No validation that api_key is non-empty, free of CRLF injection, or is not a debug placeholder. An attacker who controls a config value or header injection site could smuggle newlines into the Authorization header to pivot to header injection. Additionally, the api_key is moved through multiple owned String clones and formatted into log-capable contexts — e.g., line 1116 debug!(url = %url, "Passthrough request") and forward_to_provider_raw_reqwest line 1430 do not log secrets, but the api_key flows through .header() which, if reqwest or axum debug-logs headers (e.g., tracing integration with TRACE level), will leak the secret.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Introduce a dedicated `ApiKey(String)` newtype in src/providers/mod.rs whose Debug and Display impls redact the value (e.g., print "***sk-...xyz" with last 4 chars). All function signatures taking &str api_key should take &ApiKey instead.
  2. Validate in ApiKey::new(): reject empty strings, reject strings containing '\r', '\n', or non-ASCII bytes. Return ProviderError::InvalidResponse on violation.
  3. Replace every format!("Bearer {api_key}") with an explicit header builder that checks HeaderValue::from_str and returns an error on failure.
  4. Audit all tracing calls near the auth path: ensure no api_key appears in format args. The secret must never be in a tracing field, even a %-formatted one.
